name: Falling Chocolate Cake with Raspberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Butter 10 ramekins (or individual serving dishes) with the butter.
3. Melt chocolate and butter in a double boiler, stirring until smooth.
4. Cool the melted chocolate mixture to room temperature.
5. In a large bowl, beat sugar and eggs with an electric mixer until well combined.
6. Add flour and beat until thick and creamy, about 3 minutes.
7. Stir in the chocolate mixture until well combined and divide batter among the ramekins.
8. Place the ramekins on a baking sheet and bake in the middle of the oven for about 15 minutes, or until the cake surfaces are cracked and the centers tremble slightly.
9. Cool cakes in ramekins on a rack for 5 to 10 minutes, or until cool enough to handle.
10. Run a thin knife around the edges of the ramekins and invert warm cakes onto dessert plates.
11. Sprinkle cakes with confectioner's sugar and garnish with mint leaves.
12. Serve cakes with vanilla ice cream and raspberry sauce.
Note: For an alternative flour option, consider using pastry flour, cake flour, walnut flour, or a mixture of 3/4 cake flour and 1/4 walnut flour instead of regular all-purpose flour.
